title: transaction — published events
description: Every event implements `ShouldDispatchAfterCommit` so listeners never fire against a rolled-back write. LedgerImbalanceDetected is the CRITICAL P0 event of the module — it should NEVER fire in a healthy system; if it does, the ledger is broken + new transactions halt until an ops-authored fix lands. TransactionReversed + TransactionAmendmentRequested are the two write-critical audit signals (regulator-facing). AccountBalanceThresholdCrossed is an ops signal (accounts_receivable > $X, cash < $Y).

events.name: TransactionCreated
events.trigger: TransactionObserver.creating committed a new transactions row (status='draft', before ledger entries attach). Fires exactly once per transaction. Precedes TransactionPosted by the balancing step in the same DB transaction.

payload.transaction_number: string (TXN-YYYY-NNNNNNNN — sequential per tenant per year)
payload.reference_type: string (Invoice / Payment / Refund / Chargeback / CouponRedemption / adjustment_row)

events.name: TransactionPosted
events.trigger: Ledger entries balanced (SUM(debits) == SUM(credits) > 0) + transaction.status transitioned draft → posted + posted_at timestamp set. Terminal state — every posted transaction is IMMUTABLE.
consumers: audit::WriteToAuditLog (retention: 7 years — SOX 404; every posted transaction is regulator-visible)
cache::InvalidateAccountBalanceCache (invalidates per-account balance keys for every account touched)

payload.accounts_touched: string[] (unique list of ledger accounts touched by this transaction)

events.name: TransactionReversed
events.trigger: TransactionReverser committed a NEW transaction pointing at an existing one via source_transaction_id + reversal_reason. The reversal transaction is a first-class posted transaction with offsetting ledger entries (debit ↔ credit swapped). The ORIGINAL transaction remains posted + IMMUTABLE. This event fires on the REVERSAL transaction (not on the original — originals never change).
consumers: audit::WriteToAuditLog (retention: 7 years — regulator-critical audit trail for every ledger correction)
notifications::DispatchTransactionAmendmentAppliedNotification (finance role + admin — every reversal is regulator-visible)

payload.amount_cents: integer (positive; direction indicated by the ledger entries of the reversal)

events.name: TransactionAmendmentRequested
events.trigger: Admin invoked POST /transactions/{id}/reverse with reversal_reason='accounting_adjustment' — a manual override for correcting a ledger entry that was posted incorrectly. Distinct from the automated invoice-refund flow (which uses reversal_reason='refund'). Every fire is regulator-critical + must include a written note.
consumers: audit::WriteToAuditLog (retention: 7 years — REQUIRED for manual overrides per SOX 404)
monitoring::MetricsCollector (increments stackra.transaction.amendment_requested_total; any non-zero rate is a signal to investigate)

events.name: LedgerEntryRecorded
events.trigger: TransactionLedgerEntryObserver.creating committed a ledger entry row. Fires once per entry — a typical invoice-paid transaction fires TWICE (one debit entry + one credit entry).
consumers: cache::InvalidateAccountBalanceCache (invalidates the specific account's balance cache)

payload.debit_cents: integer (0 when this is a credit entry)
payload.credit_cents: integer (0 when this is a debit entry)

events.name: LedgerImbalanceDetected
events.trigger: CRITICAL P0. SUM(debit_cents) != SUM(credit_cents) for a transaction OR the nightly LedgerReconciler found a global tenant-account imbalance. Should NEVER fire in a healthy system — if it does, the ledger is broken + new transactions HALT until an ops-authored fix lands. Every fire pages the on-call + escalates to finance leadership + writes a critical audit row.
consumers: notifications::DispatchLedgerImbalanceDetectedNotification (cannot-opt-out — pages on-call + finance leadership + platform ops)
monitoring::MetricsCollector (increments stackra.ledger.imbalances_detected_total; every increment triggers PagerDuty P0)
transaction::HaltNewTransactionsOnImbalance (sets tenant-level flag transaction.halt_new = true until resolved)

events.name: AccountBalanceThresholdCrossed
events.trigger: The AccountBalanceCalculator computed a per-account running balance that crossed a config-defined threshold. Example: accounts_receivable > $50k (collection risk); cash < $1k (liquidity risk); refunds_owed > $10k (needs disbursement). Not a P0 — signals ops to review.

events.name: TenantOpeningBalanceRecorded
events.trigger: RecordOpeningBalanceOnTenantProvisioned hook fired on tenancy::TenantProvisioned. Creates one transaction with kind='opening_balance' + ledger entries at zero for every enabled account (or non-zero when migrating from a legacy system with existing balances). Fires exactly once per tenant.
consumers: audit::WriteToAuditLog (retention: 7 years — the tenant's ledger origin story)

payload.accounts_seeded: string[] (list of chart-of-accounts entries with entries)

events.name: TransactionCurrencyConverted
events.trigger: Fires ONLY on cross-currency transactions (transaction.currency ≠ tenant.base_currency). The ExchangeRateResolver captured a rate; base_amount_cents was computed atomically before the ledger balancing step. Downstream reports use base_amount_cents for aggregation across currencies.
consumers: audit::WriteToAuditLog (retention: 7 years — currency conversions are regulator-critical for FX audit trails)

events.name: LedgerReconciliationCompleted
events.trigger: ReconcileLedgerBalancesJob completed its nightly sweep. Reports total transactions scanned + per-account balance snapshots + any imbalances detected (fires LedgerImbalanceDetected per imbalance BEFORE this event). Every fire is expected; every scan-completed-with-imbalances-found is a P0.
consumers: audit::WriteToAuditLog (retention: 7 years — regulators love to see the nightly reconciler completing on schedule)
